Author: Jatin Prasad
Title: How to Become an Agent Engineer: The Only Roadmap You Need for Building AI Agents
Text:
Member-only story
How to Become an Agent Engineer: The Only Roadmap You Need for Building AI Agents
Learn the concepts, skills, and engineering practices behind modern agentic systems, and build real projects along the way
Artificial intelligence has entered its next phase. Not just chatbots. Not just models that predict the next word. We are now designing agents, systems that can observe, reason, plan, and take actions in the real world. This shift is as significant as moving from static websites to fully dynamic web applications. A chatbot answers questions; an agent completes tasks. It browses. It executes code. It retrieves data. It coordinates with other agents. It learns from past failures. And when done right, it can behave like a dependable digital teammate.
Yet the field of agent engineering is confusing for newcomers. There are courses on prompt engineering, libraries for workflows, scattered tutorials on RAG, and countless marketing pages promising “autonomous AI.” What beginners rarely get is a single, comprehensive roadmap, a practical guide to what you should learn, in what order, and why it matters if you want to build reliable AI agents.
This article is that guide.
